title: Connectedness in Metric Spaces: Classification and Properties
description: A flowchart demonstrating the logical hierarchy and relationships between different types of connectedness in metric spaces, showing how they lead to important results for semester-end examinations.
relationships: A space is disconnected if it can be partitioned by two non-empty, disjoint open sets U and V
A connected space is one that is not disconnected
Path-connectedness is a stronger condition that implies connectedness
Connectedness is preserved under continuous functions
The Intermediate Value Theorem is a fundamental consequence of connectedness
